h2h_summary: Jordan and Argentina have met only a handful of times in friendlies with no competitive meeting prior to this World Cup; the most recent encounter was a 1-1 draw in 1990.
team_style_home: Jordan play a compact, disciplined mid-block and rely on quick counter-attacks and transitions, using pace on the flanks — particularly via Mousa Al-Tamari — to threaten on the break.
team_style_away: Argentina deploy a fluid 4-3-3 that morphs into a 3-2-5 in build-up, using patient possession through a high-quality midfield trio and Messi as a deep-lying 'Quarterback 10' to unlock defences.
wc_history_home: Jordan are making their first-ever FIFA World Cup appearance in 2026, having qualified by defeating Oman 3-0 in June 2025 after reaching the 2023 AFC Asian Cup final and the 2025 Arab Cup final.
wc_history_away: Argentina are three-time World Cup champions (1978, 1986, 2022), entering 2026 as defending champions with Messi making a record sixth World Cup appearance across 19 tournament appearances.
group_context: Group J contains Argentina (defending champions, FIFA #2), Austria, Algeria, and debutants Jordan; Argentina are heavy favorites to top the group while Jordan, Algeria, and Austria compete fiercely for second place and potential third-place progression.
strengths_home: Disciplined defensive organization — conceded only 4 goals across all AFC qualifying matches
Dangerous counter-attacking threat through Mousa Al-Tamari's pace and creativity
Tournament resilience — runners-up at 2023 AFC Asian Cup and 2025 Arab Cup
strengths_away: World-class individual quality throughout the squad — Messi, Alvarez, Lautaro, Romero, Martínez
Exceptional defensive record — conceded just 10 goals across 18 CONMEBOL qualifying matches
Tactical flexibility under Scaloni with a fluid system adaptable to any opponent
